description: The main Piwik PRO tracking tag responsible for collecting analytics data. This tag loads the tracking library and configures data collection (page views, outlinks, downloads, content impressions, custom dimensions). Configure it after installing Piwik PRO to control how the tracker collects data.

when_to_use: Setting up or configuring the main Piwik PRO analytics tracking on a site or app
Enabling/disabling specific data collection features (outlinks, downloads, content tracking)
Configuring visitor recognition across subdomains or domains
Setting up custom dimensions for the tracking tag
Adjusting privacy and cookie settings for the tracker
common_use_cases: Standard website analytics with page view tracking
E-commerce or content sites needing outlink and download tracking
Multi-subdomain or cross-domain setups requiring visitor recognition
Sites with custom dimensions for enhanced reporting
Privacy-conscious setups with consent and cookie controls

template_options.description: Configuration for the Piwik PRO tracker. Must be present (can be empty object {} for defaults).

template_options.link_tracking.description: Enable outlink and download tracking. Outlinks take visitors outside your site; downloads track file links.
template_options.link_tracking.recommendation: Set to true for most sites to track outlinks and downloads in Analytics > Reports

template_options.content_tracking.description: Track popups and content impressions. Options: trackAllContentImpressions (all content) or trackVisibleContentImpressions (only visible content).

options.trackAllContentImpressions: Track all content impressions within a page

template_options.content_tracking.recommendation: Use for popups and content tracking; see Analytics > Reports > Content performance

template_options.cross_domain_tracking.description: Recognize visitors across domains. Passes visitor ID via pk_vid parameter in URLs. Requires domains in Administration > Sites & apps > Data collection.
template_options.cross_domain_tracking.recommendation: Enable when tracking users across multiple domains with the same tracking code

template_options.cookie_domain.description: Domain to store the visitor cookie for cross-subdomain recognition. Use string or variable reference like {{ Campaign }}.
template_options.cookie_domain.recommendation: Set to your root domain (e.g. '.example.com') to recognize visitors across subdomains

template_options.heart_beat_timer.description: Accurately measure session duration. Sends ping requests to detect when visitor leaves the last page.

structure.enabled: boolean (required) - Enable precise session duration measurement

template_options.user_id.description: User ID to connect a user's data across sources (email, username, or unique identifier). String or variable reference.
template_options.user_id.recommendation: Use for logged-in users; stored in 'User ID' dimension for segments and custom reports

template_options.secure_cookie.description: Enable secure flag on first-party cookies. Use when site is HTTPS-only.
template_options.secure_cookie.recommendation: Set to true for HTTPS sites so cookies are sent only over secure connection

template_options.visitor_cookie_timeout.description: Visitor cookie (_pk_id.*) timeout in seconds. Default is 13 months.
template_options.visitor_cookie_timeout.recommendation: Adjust for shorter/longer visitor recognition periods

template_options.session_cookie_timeout.description: Session cookie (_pk_ses.*) timeout in seconds. Default is 30 minutes.
template_options.session_cookie_timeout.recommendation: Adjust for different session duration definitions

template_options.js_error_tracking.description: Automatically track JavaScript errors as custom events (error type and page URL).
template_options.js_error_tracking.recommendation: Enable to see errors in Analytics > Reports > Custom events

template_options.track_page_view.description: Track page views automatically. Set to false to track page views manually via virtual page view tag or JavaScript.
template_options.track_page_view.recommendation: Set to false only when using SPA or manual page view tracking

template_options.piwik_custom_dimensions.description: Custom dimensions to collect. Create dimensions first in Analytics > Settings > Custom dimensions.

structure.value: string|number|boolean or variable reference - Value to set

template_options.piwik_custom_variables.description: Legacy custom variables (scope: page or visit). Prefer piwik_custom_dimensions for new setups.

basic_tracking.description: Basic Piwik PRO tracking with defaults (page views, outlinks, downloads, JS errors)
basic_tracking.prerequisite: Requires a page_view trigger. Use triggers_list() to find one.

full_data_collection.description: Tracking with outlinks, content, cross-subdomain, and precise session duration

with_custom_dimensions.description: Tracking with custom dimensions (create dimensions in Analytics first)

update_example.description: Update tracking options (e.g., enable cross-domain, add custom dimensions)

common_mistakes: Forgetting to attach a page_view trigger - the tag won't fire
Setting cookie_domain incorrectly for cross-subdomain (use leading dot: .example.com)
Enabling cross_domain_tracking without configuring domains in Administration

tag_not_firing: Verify triggers are attached: tags_list_triggers(app_id, tag_id)
Ensure page_view trigger is active and conditions match
Check consent_type matches your consent form setup
data_not_collected: Confirm track_page_view is true if expecting automatic page views
Verify template_options are correctly structured (no typos in keys)
